title: First Solar Earnings On Deck: Can Tariff Relief Fuel Profit Growth?
text: First Solar is positioned to benefit from potential tariff relief and domestic manufacturing incentives as the solar industry navigates policy shifts. Ahead of Q1 2026 earnings, market participants are weighing whether the company can maintain profit momentum amid tariff pressures. The company reported strong Q4 2025 results with $5.2 billion in annual revenue and record module sales of 17.5 GW. However, gross margin declined to 41% from 44% due to tariff costs and facility underutilization. For 2026, management projects approximately $125-135 million in net tariff headwinds but expects partial offset from Section 45X tax credit monetization worth $2.1-$2.2 billion. The company's domestic manufacturing footprint, comprising facilities in Arizona, Ohio, Alabama, and Louisiana, provides supply chain resilience and government incentive capture opportunities. First Solar's strategy focuses on leveraging CdTe thin-film technology differentiation, domestic manufacturing scale, and backlog monetization. The key driver for earnings growth will be successful CURE technology rollout, which management expects to unlock $600 million in incremental revenue through 2028. Tariff policy decisions and solar demand trends will be critical to determining whether the company can exceed current guidance.
